taxonomy: Linosyris linosyris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. Linosyris linosyris is commonly known as the Iberian bitter-thistle. Within the Asteraceae family, it is placed in the tribe Cardueae and is closely related to other thistle species. Its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and ecological adaptations.

intro: Linosyris linosyris, also known as the Narrow-leaved Horseshoe or Narrow-leaved Horseshoe Balm, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Linosyris linosyris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Linosyris linosyris is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or topical salve to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
